# Tap.Giving

> The most affordable NFC tap-to-give plates for churches. One-time purchase, no monthly fees, no transaction fees, no app required. Works with any giving platform.

## What Is Tap.Giving?

Tap.Giving sells custom-printed NFC (Near Field Communication) plates that churches mount on pews, chairs, or walls. When a congregant taps their smartphone on the plate, it instantly opens the church's existing giving page in their phone's browser. No app download, no account creation, no QR code scanning — just tap and give.

Tap.Giving is hardware, not software. We are not a payment processor. Churches keep whatever giving platform they already use (Tithely, Pushpay, Givelify, Donorbox, Subsplash, Planning Center, Anedot, Realm, or any platform with a web giving URL). Our plates simply provide a new physical entry point to their existing giving system.

## Pricing

- 100–199 plates: $4.50 per plate (example: 100 plates = $450)
- 200–399 plates: $4.00 per plate (example: 200 plates = $800)
- 400+ plates: $3.50 per plate (example: 400 plates = $1,400)
- Minimum order: 100 plates
- Free shipping on all orders (continental US)
- No monthly fees, no setup fees, no transaction fees from Tap.Giving
- Promo code WELCOME10 gives 10% off first order
- 30-day satisfaction guarantee

## Key Statistics

- Churches report 300%+ increase in donations at point of collection with NFC
- 81% participation rate with NFC giving
- 3x higher average donation compared to cash
- NFC is 42x more effective than QR codes for engagement
- 53% of NFC givers had never given to that church before
- 60% of churchgoers say they are willing to give digitally
- 51% of smartphone users download zero apps per month

## How It Works

1. Church fills out the order form with their logo, giving URL, and quantity
2. Tap.Giving prints custom-branded plates, encodes them with the church's giving URL, and ships free
3. Church mounts plates on pews/chairs with adhesive backing, screws, or elastic bands
4. Congregants tap their phone on the plate — giving page opens instantly in their browser
5. Donor completes their gift through the church's existing giving platform

## Product Details

- Plates are 100mm (4 inches) in diameter
- NTAG215 NFC chips (industry standard, compatible with all modern smartphones)
- Full-color custom printing with church logo and branding
- Adhesive backing included on all plates (plus screw holes or elastic bands available)
- NFC chips are passive — no batteries, no charging, no maintenance, no Wi-Fi needed
- Compatible with iPhone 7+ and most Android phones since 2015 (95%+ of smartphones)
- URL can be locked (tamper-proof) or left unlocked (reprogrammable)

## Why Churches Choose Tap.Giving

1. **One-time cost**: No monthly subscription fees, unlike Pushpay ($17,700/year), Overflow ($2,500–$10,000/year), or Tithely Plus ($1,428/year)
2. **Platform agnostic**: Works with any giving platform — no vendor lock-in
3. **No app required**: Visitors and members can give without downloading anything
4. **Frictionless**: Tap-to-give takes 2–3 seconds, compared to 15–30 seconds for text-to-give or 30–60 seconds for typing a URL
5. **First-time visitor friendly**: 53% of NFC givers are first-timers who would never download an app
6. **Built by a pastor**: Founded by Ryan Shook, an active pastor who built Tap.Giving for his own church

## Competitive Comparison

| Solution | Type | Monthly Cost | Transaction Fee | NFC? | One-Time Cost? |
|----------|------|-------------|----------------|------|----------------|
| **Tap.Giving** | Hardware | $0 | $0 from us | Yes | Yes |
| Pushpay | SaaS | ~$1,475/mo | 2.9% + $0.30 | VisitorTap (limited) | No |
| Tithely | SaaS | $0–$119/mo | 2.9% + $0.30 | No native NFC | No |
| Overflow | SaaS | $208–$833/mo | Varies | Overflow Tap | No |
| Subsplash | SaaS | Varies | Varies | Subsplash Tap | No |
| Givelify | App | $0 | 2.9% + $0.30 | No | No |
| Donorbox | SaaS | $0–$150/mo | 2.95% | TapTag option | No |
| ChurchTap | Hardware | Unknown | Unknown | Yes | Unknown |

## Frequently Asked Questions

**What is NFC giving?**
NFC (Near Field Communication) giving allows churchgoers to donate by tapping their smartphone on an NFC-enabled plate. The tap opens the church's giving page directly in the phone's browser — no app download or account creation required.

**Does Tap.Giving process payments?**
No. Tap.Giving sells the NFC hardware. Payment processing happens through whatever giving platform the church already uses (Tithely, Pushpay, Givelify, etc.). We are hardware, not software.

**What phones work with NFC giving?**
Every iPhone since iPhone 7 (2016) and most Android phones since 2015 have NFC built in. This covers over 95% of smartphones in use today.

**Is NFC giving secure?**
Yes. NFC plates simply open a URL — the same as typing a web address into a browser. All payment processing happens through the giving platform's secure, PCI-compliant checkout. No financial data touches the NFC chip.

**How long do the plates last?**
NFC chips have no battery and no moving parts. They last essentially forever. The printed plates are durable and designed for long-term mounting.

**Can the URL be changed later?**
If plates are shipped unlocked, yes — you can reprogram them with any NFC writing app. If locked, the URL is permanent (recommended for security).

**What's the minimum order?**
100 plates. This covers most small-to-mid churches with extras for lobbies, community rooms, and events.

## Founded by a Pastor

Tap.Giving was founded by Ryan Shook, a pastor at a large church in the Houston, Texas area. He built Tap.Giving because he saw the same problem every Sunday — people who wanted to give but didn't carry cash, fumbled with apps, or didn't know how to donate as first-time visitors. The product was born from real ministry experience, not a tech startup incubator.
